title: BCCL's masterstroke: Amlabad mine to be a lifeline for the steel sector, energy security for the next two decades
titleHi: BCCL का मास्टरस्ट्रोकः स्टील सेक्टर के लिए संजीवनी बनेगी अमलाबाद खदान, अगले 2 दशकों तक देश को मिलेगी ऊर्जा सुरक्षा
summary: On the Dhanbad-Bokaro border, BCCL's Amlabad underground mine, holding about 6.2 million tonnes (62 lakh tonnes) of prime coking coal, is being readied to restart under a revenue-sharing model, with production seen possible for two to three decades to support India's steel industry.

title: BCCL Amlabad Mine: this Dhanbad mine will be a game-changer for the steel industry, yielding 'black gold' for 25 years
titleHi: BCCL Amlabad Mine: स्टील इंडस्ट्री के लिए गेमचेंजर बनेगी धनबाद की यह खदान, 25 साल तक उगलेगी 'काला सोना'
summary: BCCL CMD Manoj Kumar Agarwal reviewed the Amlabad underground project in Eastern Jharia, which holds around 6.2 million tonnes (62 lakh tonnes) of prime coking coal. The washery grade-1 and grade-2 coking coal is expected to serve the domestic steel industry over a 25-year production horizon.

title: Amlabad colliery to be operational within three months: CMD
titleHi: अमलाबाद कोलियरी तीन माह में होगी चालू: सीएमडी
summary: BCCL CMD Manoj Kumar Agarwal directed that the Amlabad underground mine, closed for about a decade and a half and allotted to Mangalam Coalfield Pvt Ltd in 2024 under a 25-year revenue-sharing MDO contract, be made operational within three months, and called for statutory approvals including environmental clearance to be fast-tracked.
sensitiveWhy: Article frames the restart directive alongside the CMD's remarks about curbing coal smuggling in the area.

title: BCCL CMD reviews production, dispatch and monsoon preparedness in East Jharia Area
summary: During an East Jharia Area review, BCCL's CMD visited the proposed Amlabad underground mine, planned for operation through the Mine Developer and Operator (MDO) route under a revenue-sharing model, and directed officials to fast-track statutory and administrative approvals, including environmental clearance.

title: Company must employ local workers or we will block the gate: joint front
titleHi: स्थानीय मजूदरों को काम पर रखें कंपनी, नहीं तो गेट करेंगे जाम : संयुक्त मोर्चा
summary: After a truck loaded with iron was intercepted by guards at the Amlabad BCCL colliery (operated by Mangalam Coalfield Ltd), a local joint front met at Amlabad and demanded that the company hire local workers, warning it would block the gate otherwise and seeking a police inquiry into the seized iron.
sensitiveWhy: Local protest/agitation with a gate-blockade threat, and a dispute over seized iron at the colliery.

title: All formalities completed to start Amlabad colliery in MDO mode
titleHi: आमलाबाद कोलियरी को एमडीओ मोड में चालू करने की सारी प्रकिया पूरी
summary: Amlabad colliery in Bokaro's Chandankiyari segment, shut for about 15 years, is set to restart in MDO mode with mining by Mangalam Consortium, which received the Letter of Intent for the roughly Rs 250-crore project. The company pays BCCL 4.1% per-tonne revenue share over a 25-year term, with production then expected around March-April 2024.

title: Amlabad colliery was closed when the guide rope of No. 2 chute broke
titleHi: दो नंबर चानक का गाइड रस्सा टूटने पर बंद हो गयी थी अमलाबाद कोलियरी
summary: BCCL's Amlabad colliery, shut on 29 February 2008 after the guide rope of its No. 2 chute broke, holds over 50 million tonnes of high-quality coking coal. Its workforce fell from around 3,000 at nationalisation to a few dozen maintenance staff, hitting the surrounding local economy.

title: Amlabad colliery to reopen after 16 years, production to start within a year
titleHi: 16 वर्ष बाद खुलेगी अमलाबाद कोलियरी, एक वर्ष के भीतर शुरू होगा उत्पादन
summary: Officials of Mangalam Coalfield Pvt Ltd and BCCL inspected the long-closed Amlabad colliery, which is to run in MDO mode under a 25-year arrangement. With cleanup and repair under way, production is expected within a year, bringing direct and indirect local employment in the Chandankiyari area of Bokaro.

title: Production to start soon from closed Amlabad colliery; outsourcing company Mangalam wins mining contract on revenue sharing
titleHi: बंद अमलाबाद कोलियरी से जल्द शुरू होगा उत्पादन, आउटसोर्सिंग कंपनी मंगलम को रेवेन्यू शेयरिंग पर मिला खनन का ठेका
summary: The BCCL board approved awarding the closed Amlabad colliery in the Eastern Jharia (IJE) area to Mangalam for coal extraction in MDO mode for 25 years on a 4.1% revenue-sharing basis. The board also cleared closure plans for the Kusunda and Loyabad mines.

title: 106-year-old Amlabad colliery to be operational after 14 years
titleHi: 14 साल बाद चालू होगी 106 साल पुरानी आमलाबाद कोलियरी
summary: BCCL began the process to restart its 106-year-old Amlabad colliery in Eastern Jharia, closed since 29 February 2008 after a No. 2 chute guide-rope failure. The mine, holding around 40 million tonnes of prime coking coal, is to be run in MDO mode, with contractors inspecting the site.

title: BCCL's Amlabad colliery has been shut since 2008
titleHi: 2008 से बंद पड़ी है बीसीसीएल की अमलाबाद कोलियरी
summary: BCCL's only colliery in Bokaro's Chandankiyari block, Amlabad, closed on 29 February 2008 after decades of operation, leaving the area's economy subdued. Its workforce had fallen from about 2,400 to roughly a hundred maintenance staff.

title: BCCL to restart six closed underground mines of Dhanbad; tender floated, Amlabad first on the list
titleHi: धनबाद में फिर से चालू होगी BCCL की छह बंद अंडरग्राउंड माइंस, जारी किया टेंडर
summary: BCCL's Contract Management Cell floated a tender to reopen six long-closed underground mines under the mine developer & operator (MDO) model, naming Amlabad first among them — the tender process that would later award the Amlabad revival to Mangalam Coalfield on a revenue-sharing basis.

title: BCCL to earn a 4.1% revenue share from Amlabad; coal seen selling near ₹21,000 a tonne
titleHi: बीसीसीएल को अमलाबाद से 4.1 प्रतिशत मुनाफा, स्टील उद्योग को मिलेगा बल
summary: BCCL's CMD reviewed the Amlabad project, which holds about 6.2 million tonnes of prime coking coal. Under the revenue-sharing model BCCL earns a 4.1% share, with the coking coal strengthening the domestic steel industry and creating local jobs.

title: Amlabad's closed health unit to reopen — Mangalam begins the revival
titleHi: अमलाबाद की बंद हेल्थ यूनिट फिर चालू होगी, कंपनी ने शुरू की पहल
summary: Mangalam Coalfield is reviving Amlabad's long-derelict health unit so workers and thousands of nearby villagers can again get free primary treatment locally, instead of travelling to Chas or Jharia. Director Prashalya Agarwal said health care is being restored alongside coal production.

title: Four closed collieries in Bokaro district set to restart in 2025 — Amlabad first
titleHi: नए साल में बोकारो जिले की बंद पड़ी चार कोलियरियां होंगी चालू
summary: A district feature on four long-closed Bokaro collieries being brought back to life, with Amlabad — shut since 2008 — listed first, and Mangalam engaged to reopen it on a revenue-sharing basis.

title: Amlabad to reopen after 16 years; a new Damodar bridge will link it directly to Dhanbad
titleHi: 16 साल बाद खुलेगी अमलाबाद कोलियरी, 30 साल बाद दामोदर नदी पर बन रहा पुल, धनबाद से सीधे जुड़ जाएगा
summary: As the colliery is revived under Mangalam, a long-awaited bridge over the Damodar river is being built — reconnecting the area directly to Dhanbad and bringing the Bhaura market and local economy back to life.

title: “Coal mining at Amlabad to begin within a year”: company directors and BCCL inspect the colliery
titleHi: अमलाबाद में 1 साल में शुरू होगा कोयला खनन: निदेशक
summary: Mangalam Coalfield and BCCL officials inspected the closed Amlabad colliery and appealed for villagers' cooperation, saying production could begin within a year and that the mine would run in MDO mode for 25 years.
